programmes.show_name: East of the Sun and West of the Moon
programmes.show_description: 4 Extra Debut. A Norwegian fairy tale about a polar bear who is really a bewitched prince. Starring Martin Jarvis and Helen Worth.
As death threatens to separate legendary film duo Laurel and Hardy, Stan tries to say things left unsaid. Stars Tom Courtenay.
4 Extra Debut. Leading actor Nicholas Craig, recalls Jacobean drama and playing footballer Stanley Matthews. Stars Nigel Planer. From 1989.

programmes.show_name: Poetry Extra: Poetry Please - Pot Luck
programmes.show_description: A 4 Extra listener requests Poetry Please: Pot Luck featuring the poem 'The Drunk on the Train'.  From 2015.
4 Extra Debut. A revealing interview with the "naughty boy of the BBC" on his local radio station. Presented by Iain Mann. From 1976.
As young Titus approaches his destiny in Gormenghast, he faces the rise of the ruthlessly ambitious Steerpike. Stars Sting.
4 Extra Debut. Alistair Cooke re-records his first ever Letter from America from 1946, and reflects on its post war context. From 1996.
Leslie Thomas, Maureen Lipman and Frank Thornton tell the stories. Tim Brooke-Taylor tries to spot which is a lie. From 1986.
Raucous and roguish sketch comedy. Starring Robert Bathurst, Emma Thompson, Jimmy Mulville and Rory McGrath. From 1980.
4 Extra Debut. John Walters with his goldfish and cat take over the airwaves and let Frank Sinatra sing the football results. From 1992.
1/5. Miss Marple senses danger at a friend's manor house school for wayward boys. Agatha Christie's whodunnit stars June Whitfield.
1/5. The anonymous English hero is now in a German jail. But there's been an air raid. Sequel to Rogue Male read by Michael Jayston.
6/6. Fulton and Margaret prepare to rededicate their vows. Stars Robin Bailey and Doreen Mantle. From June 1980.
13/20. Butcher Jones is short of meat, so Walker arranges a special off-ration supply. Adapted from BBC TV with Clive Dunn. From 1975.
1/4. In attempting to write Sigmund Freud's biography, Boswell finds it's Freud asking all the questions. With Miles Jupp. From 2015.
1/5. While five-year old William fishes in the castle moat, his older brother's seizures are diagnosed. Memoir read by Dan Stevens.
1/5. Winter 1913 finds Frank Reid, the English owner of a Moscow printing company, abandoned by his wife. Narrated by Clare Higgins.
1/6. Phil Wang and Susie Dent challenge guests to invent new words and help create the new ultimate Unspeakable dictionary. With guests Olga Koch, Aurie Styla and Tim Vine.
4 Extra Debut. Alistair Cooke reflects on the changes he has witnessed marking his 500th Letter from America. From 1957.
Christian reflection and music with Fr Philip Blackledge of Holy Trinity Scottish Episcopal Church in Melrose. Reading: Matthew 26:36-41.
